seoTitle: Goodar vs Goodna — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Goodar and Goodna. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Goodar and Goodna.
heading: Goodar vs Goodna — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Goodar, Queensland and Goodna, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Goodar has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Goodna (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Goodar or Goodna safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Goodar has a flood risk score of 45/100, while Goodna scores 48/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Goodar scores 21/100 for bushfire risk. Goodna scores 51/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Goodar or Goodna?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Goodar has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.25% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
